Container.Name: brigata-studio2go
Container.Overview: A private, self-hosted Brigata AI agent + Studio that runs entirely on your Unraid box. No cloud dependency and no inbound connection required — you reach it on your LAN. The only outbound traffic is to the Anthropic API for the model (your key). Password login; embedded Postgres persisted to appdata.
Container.Description: Self-hosted single-tenant Brigata Studio (Studio-to-Go). One admin (password login), one workspace, one in-process agent. Set an admin password and your Anthropic API key, map a data volume, and open the WebUI on port 3030. Optionally mount /mnt/user and the Docker socket to let the agent see and manage your homelab (e.g. set up Frigate/NVR) — both are opt-in and powerful, so enable only if you want write access.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port the Studio web UI + API listen on

Config.Name: Data (Postgres + config)
Config.Description: Persistent data — the embedded database and generated config. Survives updates.

Config.Name: Admin password
Config.Description: The login password for the admin user (set on first boot)

Config.Name: Anthropic API key
Config.Description: sk-ant-api03-... or sk-ant-oat01-... — used only when the agent runs

Config.Name: Homelab access (optional)
Config.Description: OPTIONAL: mount your shares so the agent can read/manage files (e.g. Frigate config). Remove to keep it isolated.

Config.Name: Docker control (optional, powerful)
Config.Description: OPTIONAL + ROOT-EQUIVALENT: lets the agent create/manage containers (e.g. set up an NVR). Only enable if you want that.
